skill.name: developer-growth-analysis
skill.description: Analyzes your recent Claude Code chat history to identify coding patterns, development gaps, and areas for improvement, curates relevant learning resources from HackerNews, and automatically sends a personalized growth report to your Slack DMs.
skill.summary: Analyzes recent Claude Code history to produce developer growth feedback, learning resources, and Slack-ready reports.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: The static backtick and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown examples, inline code formatting, and prose. The material risk is privacy-related: the skill instructs the agent to read local Claude Code chat history, including pasted content, search external HackerNews resources, and send a derived report to Slack.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Claude Code History Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read ~/.claude/history.jsonl and process user messages, projects, timestamps, and pasted contents. This can expose proprietary code, credentials, internal project names, or private troubleshooting context unless users explicitly consent and redact sensitive data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and fields are stated directly, including pastedContents. The privacy risk follows from the stated data source and scope.

medium_findings.title: External Sharing of Derived Development Data
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to search HackerNews using queries derived from improvement areas and to send the complete report to Slack DMs through Rube MCP. The intent appears legitimate, but project details or sensitive summaries may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require external HackerNews searches and Slack delivery. I found no evidence of malicious intent, but the data movement is clear.

medium_findings.title: No Explicit Redaction Step Before Report Delivery
medium_findings.description: The report is generated from chat history that may include pasted code and then delivered as a complete report to Slack. No evidence found of a required secret scan, redaction step, or user approval checkpoint before sending.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data source and delivery step are explicit, and the reviewed instructions do not include redaction or approval requirements. The exact report contents depend on runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Blocks
low_findings.description: The static Ruby/shell backtick execution alerts are caused by Markdown code fences, inline field names, and example prompts. No executable shell command or Ruby code execution pattern is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples or inline code labels after reviewing the file. I did not find command execution syntax or instructions to execute those strings.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to prose, headings, report examples, dates, links, and learning-resource text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation in the cited file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows natural language and sample report text at the cited locations. There is no cryptographic API usage or algorithm selection to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Wording
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to guidance about identifying challenges in the user's recent work. It does not instruct the agent to inventory the host system, enumerate processes, or collect machine metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a developer coaching checklist and refers to user challenges. No host reconnaissance instruction appears at that location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reads Hidden Local History File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs access to ~/.claude/history.jsonl, a hidden local file that can contain private prompts and pasted code. This is a legitimate feature goal but requires user consent and careful minimization.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden file path and fields are explicitly documented. The privacy implication is direct.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sends Generated Report to Slack
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to send the complete report through Slack DMs. This transfers derived local development information to an external service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Slack delivery instructions are explicit and include sending the report content. The sensitivity depends on what the report includes.

content.user_title: Analyze Developer Growth From Claude Code History
content.value_statement: Developers often miss repeated patterns in their daily coding work. This skill reviews recent Claude Code history and creates focused growth advice with learning resources.
actual_capabilities: Reads recent Claude Code chat history from the local history file.
Filters activity to a recent 24 to 48 hour reporting window.
Identifies technologies, project types, recurring challenges, and improvement areas.
Generates a structured developer growth report with strengths and action items.
Searches HackerNews through Rube MCP for learning resources tied to improvement areas.
Sends the completed report to the user's Slack direct messages when connected.
limitations: Requires access to local Claude Code history, which may contain sensitive pasted content.
Requires Rube MCP and Slack authorization for external search and report delivery.
Recommendations depend on the quality and recency of available chat history.
The skill does not define a required secret redaction step before Slack delivery.

use_cases.title: Weekly Growth Review
use_cases.description: Review recent coding work and identify one or two skills to improve next week.

use_cases.title: Mentor Discussion Prep
use_cases.description: Create an evidence-based summary of recent challenges, strengths, and learning needs.

use_cases.title: Focused Learning Plan
use_cases.description: Turn repeated debugging or design issues into a short reading list and action plan.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Recent Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my developer growth from my recent Claude Code chats and summarize the main improvement areas.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple growth report.

prompt_templates.title: Today Only Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my work from today, identify repeated challenges, and suggest practical next steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of one day of activity.

prompt_templates.title: Learning Resource Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my recent coding history, identify three skill gaps, and find relevant HackerNews resources for each area.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that includes resource curation.

prompt_templates.title: Slack Delivery Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a developer growth report for the last 48 hours, include action items, and send it to my Slack DMs after I approve the summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with external delivery and user approval.

output_examples.input: Analyze my developer growth from my recent chats.
output_examples.output: A report that summarizes recent projects, lists prioritized improvement areas, highlights strengths, and recommends focused action items.

output_examples.input: Find learning resources for the problems I repeated this week.
output: A short explanation of each recurring challenge.
Two or three relevant HackerNews discussions for each improvement area.
A suggested study order based on expected impact.

output_examples.input: Send my growth report to Slack.
output_examples.output: A Slack-ready version of the report split into summary, improvements, strengths, actions, and resource sections.

best_practices: Review and approve the report before sending it to Slack.
Remove secrets, customer names, and proprietary code from chat history when possible.
Run the analysis on a regular cadence so trends are easier to compare.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill on chat history that contains credentials or regulated data.
Do not send reports to Slack channels or users beyond the intended recipient.
Do not treat the recommendations as performance evaluation without human review.

faq.question: What data does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes recent Claude Code chat history, including project names, prompts, timestamps, and pasted content.

faq.question: Does it need Slack access?
faq.answer: Slack access is only needed when you want the completed report sent to your direct messages.

faq.question: Can it work without HackerNews search?
faq.answer: It can still summarize patterns, but curated external learning resources require the Rube MCP search workflow.

faq.question: Is this safe for private company code?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill may process pasted code and send derived summaries to Slack unless you review and redact first.

faq.question: How often should I run it?
faq.answer: Weekly use is a practical cadence because it gives enough activity for meaningful patterns.

faq.question: Does it replace a mentor or code review?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured self-review material that can support mentoring and code review discussions.
